Video.summary: The RM Auction Company has worked with Dale before as a buyer, seller, historian, and caretaker at the prestigious Lee Hartung auction. But this event is no last minute rush to define one manÆs treasures. This is a well calculated, time consuming, multi-media, and multi motorcycle and prized art deal.
Video.title: Second Time's a Charm

Video.summary: ōThe Century Raceö: Dale believes he found the bike that will help him beat a rival in an annual race of 100 year old motorcycles. But will it run?
Video.title: The Century Race

Video.summary: A California collector gambles on a big payday from his Crocker/Caddy combo.
Video.title: Doubling Down

Video.summary: For Dale and Matt sometimes the dotted line to treasure splits and heads in opposite directions. In this edition of WhatÆs in the Barn, time is thrust to the forefront of this multi-state, long distance, north, south search. Timeless and priceless 110 year old bikes, antique cars, airplanes, and the infamous Egberts 1930 neon Harley-Davidson sign that coined the phrase ōRide a Winnerö are high on this father and sonÆs list of ōmust havesö. The clock ticks, both literally and figuratively, on one rare time piece and two old-timerÆs cars, bikes, and automobiles.
Video.title: Time is Not On Their Side

Image.alt: Time is Not On Their Side
Time is Not On Their Side
Time is Not On Their Side

Video.summary: Promoters of the prestigious Amelia Island Concours have called on Dale to develop a show and field bikes for an ōOrphan Bikeö show. They have defined orphan bike as extremely rare, that aren't a Harley nor Indian machines.
Video.title: Orphan Bikes

Video.summary: Home Depot Senior Vice President, Gordon Erickson, is a huge fan of Wheels Through Time. He is also a huge supporter of the Wounded Warrior program. For years the hardware giant has been donating goods and services to help returning servicemen with their fix-it-up projects. Now they would like to go one step further and build a WWII military bike to raise awareness and funds. Only problem is where to get one? Enter Dale and an exhaustive, time sensitive, cross country search for a rare military machine.
Video.title: Wounded Warrior Project

Video.summary: Big Time Motorcycle Auction
Video.title: Vegas Payday

Video.summary: Built in the mid-60Æs by eccentric engineer ōWild Billö Gelbke, the Road Dog was, and still is, the largest motorcycle ever built. It utilized aircraft tubing, Corvette disc brakes, and a Chevy truck differential to keep it stable. The one-off creation disappeared when Gelbke did in 1978. Outflanked once when the bike was spotted and sold to a private collector in 1995, Dale has vowed to locate the machine and make Wheels Through Time its final resting place.
Video.title: Biker Lore

Video.summary: Matt is out for revenge. Last year Dale beat him badly at the Annual Vintage bike race in Barber, Alabama. But this year there is a whole new tale to tell. With just days until the race - Dale is secretly struggling to get the bike he beat Matt with last year up and working. Matt struggles just to locate a 100-year old bike. Matt's prayers are answered thanks to an old Catholic Priest he located who has possession of a bike. The priest might let Matt have the bike to race - but there are conditions. The toughest one: Don't tell Dale you have it. Can Matt afford to buy the bike and get it running in time for the race without Dale seeing it? Will Dale even get his broken bike started? And if they ever get to race, will Matt exact his revenge?
Video.title: Don't Tell Dale

Video.summary: An family farm in the mid-west contains houses, barns, outbuildings, trailers, grain silos and buses full of old stuff and perhaps the biggest stash of undiscovered old motorcycles in the country. The collection was put together by their unnamed father. Seller agrees to have Dale broker rare Excelsior for high-end client. Dale has the buyer, but will he outbid his own client for the exclusive find?
Video.title: Diamond in Dodge City

Video.summary: ō12hrs. & Runningö: Dale turns a surprise 60th birthday party into a challenge to his guests to start 120 of the rarest motorcycles in the world.
Video.title: 12 Hours and Running

Video.summary: A cross-country race to lay claim to some of the worldÆs most sought after works specials.
Video.title: Hidden Hillclimbers

Video.summary: A vague tip leads Matt and Dale into a WWII era military warehouse and beyond.
Video.title: Peashooter

Video.summary: A thousand bulging barns of choppers, bobbers, and board trackers are emptied and on display in the Quad Cities.
Video.title: Davenport Swap

Video.summary: ōYes, Dale there is a 100 Year Old Harleyö: Dale hopes his labor of love becomes a prized possession when a six-figure Harley goes to auction.
Video.title: 100 Year Old Harley

Video.summary: ōA Motorcycle Crime Storyö: The horrific tale of a double murder over money and motorcycles.
Video.title: Motorcycle Crime Story

Video.summary: Nick Rutter teaches how to restore interior plastic trim, glass cleaning, convertible top care and more
Video.title: Speciality Car Care

Video.summary: Nick Rutter teaches how to repair common scratches in automotive paint
Video.title: Paint Scratch Repair

Video.summary: Bryan takes a trip to Huntington Beach, CA to learn some new detailing tips from the pros.
Video.title: Open House at Surf City

Video.summary: Kevin and Bryan take a Lexus IS250 and add the latest FSport performance products from front to back - taking IS performance to the next level.
Video.title: F Sport Performance Upgrades

Video.summary: Ken Schrader drops off a 2004 Corvette that is in need of some TLC.
Video.title: 2004 Corvette Project

Video.summary: Kevin calls on his friends at Green Earth Technologies to learn about G-Oil and see how it is made.
Video.title: G-Oil

Video.summary: Kevin and Bryan throw a couple hundred horses at a 2010 Camaro pace car with a new supercharger.
Video.title: Pace Setter

Video.summary: Kevin and Bryan upgrade the Federated Corvette with new suspension components.
Video.title: 2004 Corvette Gets Shocked

Video.summary: High MPG doesnÆt have to be boring. Kevin and Bryan add a few of their own touches to a new Prius.
Video.title: Hot Hybrid

Video.summary: Kevin heads out to California for a special car show celebrating the spirit of the car collector.
Video.title: Car Collector Appreciation Day

Video.summary: A 1970 Hemi Charger is restored.
Video.title: HEMI FK5 Alive

Video.summary: The 1970 Hemi Charger project is completed in the season finale.
Video.title: Pitch and Rides

Video.summary: The Graveyard Ghouls return with the final assembly of the legendary General Lee which so happens to also be the official screen-used, record holding, freeway jump-car from the 2005 Dukes of Hazzard film.
Video.title: General Assemble Lee

Video.summary: Mark isolates himself in the machine-shop and begins the assembly of the 440 4BBL for the 1970 Plum Crazy Challenger previously owned by a former cast member.
Video.title: Put Up Your Dukes

Video.summary: ItÆs a delivery day at Graveyard Carz! We jump back and show you every moment in the restoration of our legendary, screen-used, record holding, General Lee from the 2005 Dukes of Hazzard film. It all builds to a reveal the Duke Boys would be proud of.
Video.title: General Deliver Lee

Video.summary: With all the distractions out of the way, Mark returns to the machine-ship to finish the 440 4BBL for the 1970 Plum Crazy Challenger while master-assembler Dave begins working on the 1970 Lemon-Twist Roadrunner.
Video.title: Inner Workings of a Victim

Video.summary: With Mark out of the shop looking at a potential new location for a Mopar car-dealership, the Graveyard Ghouls begin the drivetrain assemblyÆs for a 1970 Lime Light Tribute Superbird and our 1970 Plum Crazy Challenger.
Video.title: Dueling Drivetrains

Video.summary: Mark the the Graveyard Ghouls race against the clock to finish up some last-minute tasks on their various Mopar restorations before traveling north to judge Little Creek CasinoÆs epic car show.
Video.title: Getting Schooled
